desc:
Zigzagging its way through the grass which stands several feet tall, the trail
cannot avoid the disrupting power of ancient roots. Decayed shrubs and fallen
trees line the trail. The threat of the forest is ever present, with its oak,
beech and fir uneasily following every move of possible intruders. The trail
marches on to the east and west.
name: Old Forest Trail

desc:
Mirkwood grows dense in the north, completely engulfed in darkness. The forest
is murky and its vegetation leaves little space for exploration. Weathered and
old, this road slithers through stumps and tall grass to the east and west. At
the feet of a great oak a bear's den has been pillaged and destroyed.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The trees grow dense to the south, growing tall on a forested ridge. To the
north the slope leads downwards into a pitch black forest, while to the east and
west the dwarven-made road continues its uneasy path. Alongside the forest road
lie burned-out shrubs and trees, with young plants growing up out of the fallen
and withered remains. The wood appears to sway slowly back and forth, hovering
over the trail, closely watching all of its immediate surroundings, awaiting new
travellers.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road shows signs of neglect, as if rarely travelled and never maintained.
Dark green bushes, vines and shrubs lie spread across the forest floor. In some
places roots have extended their arms from the ground, disrupting the once-level
surface of the road. The forest with its gnarled, twisting trees grows very
dense both in the north and south, while the ravaged road continues to the east
and west.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Beech and oak closely line the road, warily guarding the secrets of the forest.
Felled trees and their gnarled branches lie broken next to the pathway, most of
them heavily clad with fungi and mosses. The road itself leads south and west.
Vile darkness is spread across the forest, aided by the heavy forest canopy
which blocks out the natural light. The air is still, but somehow full of the
uncanny silence of Mirkwood.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
A blackened, dense soil forms the foundation for what once could have been a
major road through the forest. For centuries the wilderness has fought its way
onto the pathway, covering it in mosses and shrubs, sometimes disrupting it with
the roots of the great trees lining it on either side. The road cuts its way
through the heavy forest to north and east.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
What looks like an ancient, mostly overgrown thoroughfare spans from east to
west. Plain grass covers most of the ground, while dark, twisted and rotten
trees tower on both the south and north side of the road. A muddy path can be
discerned closer to the southern half of the road, and small footprints are
obvious in a few wet muddy spots.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
An overgrown road stretches through this forest from east to west. Waist-high
shrubs grow here and there along the road. Great trees stand beside the road
like enormous, blackened guardians; shielding against the daylight from the sky
above and cloaking what lies beyond them with their massive, tangled bulk. The
road must see some use these days, as a path of trampled grass can be seen at
the southern edge of the road.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Thick dark trees line this partly overgrown forest road. The massive oaks and
beeches that grow here reach over and across the path, blocking out the sky
above. A small niche on the northern side of the road looks like it has been
used as a resting place for travellers recently: a circle of stones mark the
spot that has been used as a fireplace.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
As the road approaches a turn, the forest grows thicker and branches entangle
themselves across and above the road, creating an impregnable roof. Strings of
spider webs hang from a few branches, and trees on both sides of the road are
covered in silky webs. High grass covers most of the roadway, but what looks
like a trampled path through the grass meanders along the southern edge.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
The road here bends from south to west, striking boldly through a dark, musty
and rotten alley of giant oak trees. Above, an impenetrable ceiling of boughs
and old cobwebs obscures the sky. The leaves of the canopy are withered and
infested with silky webs and small spiders. The ground is covered in clumps of
clay and patches of pale, yellow grass like thin fur on a giant beast.
name: Bend in the Old Forest Road

desc:
The road bends in from the north and deflects towards the east as it makes its
way through the dense, dark forest. Withered dead grass covers the roadway and a
few dried-up shrubs grow close to the southern tree line. A small muddy patch
stretches from the north to the east, parallel to the roadway: it looks as if
the road is being used sometimes, after all. An old log lies across the road,
preventing anyone from passing by on a carriage or a cart. Branches and boughs
reach in from all sides above, creating a roof-like structure interwoven with
old, decaying cobwebs.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
This overgrown roadway stretches out from east to west. It is covered in thick,
waist-high grass, peppered with dense, thorny shrubs and the occasional oak or
beech sapling. North of the road stands a massive wall of mighty oaks, whose
thick boughs reach over and across the road. South of the road, there is a
seemingly younger forest where beech and oak grow in varied and somewhat eerie
shapes. At a casual glance it looks like the beeches have yellow, black-eyed
faces and the oaks have snarling, fanged mouths - upon closer inspection it can
be discerned that it is just the way they have grown.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Branches and boughs from trees on both sides of this overgrown road reach over
it, creating an impregnable roof of dark leaves and foul, worm-scarred branches.
The road itself is mostly covered in pale grasses and clumps of withered, dead
shrubs. A small, well-trampled path follows the roadway along its southern edge,
maybe a trace of whoever has been using this forsaken road recently.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Long ago, someone cleared a large patch of forest in this area, and created a
camp alongside the Old Forest Road. Looking around, one can find a number of
traces of whoever used this place: an old well was dug here, some rocks are
arranged like a fire place, rotting planks and rusted nails litter the ground,
and a broken old wooden bucket tied to a decrepit rope lies next to the well.
Whoever it was who once used this place as a base seems to be long gone.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Enormous, sinister-looking oaks line both sides of this dark, forsaken road. On
the southern side, a thick gaggle of black roots and vines snakes up onto the
roadway; it is as though they were actively seeking to suffocate the road and
cover it entirely. A thin trail can be discerned on the northern half of the
roadway, where heavy feet have plodded a path through the yellow, waist-high
grass.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Oak and beech are the two dominant tree species in this dark and shadowy forest.
The canopy above blocks out all chance of ever seeing the sky. A small pool of
water can be seen along the northern side of the overgrown roadway, its surface
covered with rotting leaves and absolutely quivering with the activities of
mosquito larvae. A short, squat tree grows in the middle of the roadway,
surrounded by wild thorny bushes and dense grass.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
At this part of the Old Forest Road, canopy does not hang over the road,
allowing a clear view to the open sky. The road continues its journey east and
west through Taur-e-Ndaedelos, the Sindarin name for Mirkwood, meaning Forest of
Great Fear. On both sides of the road, heavy foliage and dark bushes warn
travellers to stay on the beaten path.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
In an almost surprising fashion, the road dog-legs into a tight turn here. Dense
vegetation gives no easy entrance to Mirkwood proper as the sides of the road
are fully covered with thorns and brambles. Lurching oak and birch lean out over
the road from above, heavy grey-green mosses dangling from them.
name: Turn in the Road

desc:
The Old Forest Road bends north-east underneath a canopy of thick limbs that
almost completely block out any external light. Strange sounds emanate from the
woods on all sides of the road and the feeling that something or someone is
watching is somewhat disquieting. Old leaves and dried moss crackle underfoot,
snapping and cracking loudly in the tense silence.
name: Elbow in the Road

desc:
The road is clear and easy to follow here, despite the thorn-bushes and thistles
that are slowly regaining the area. Debris fallen from the trees litters the
road, making the going slow and difficult. On both sides of the road, black
trees are stretching towards it, as if to drive away the travellers who do not
belong here.
name: Old Forest Road

desc:
Greedy branches interlace overhead to form a dark canopy above the road. Leaves
crunch on the ground loudly underfoot, and the tension of Mirkwood fills the
air. Long, putrid mosses sway gently just overhead as creatures rustle and
scurry through the nearby woods.
name: Dark Way in the Road

desc:
Here the dark trees bordering the road have managed to reach across to
intertwine high overhead, forming a living ceiling of sorts that completely
blocks the sunlight. Long, hairy mosses in hues of grey, green, and white dangle
down like ancient beards left unshaven. The feeling of being watched is
heightened by the canopy above.
name: Road under the Trees

desc:
The forest closes in from all directions; watching, listening, disdaining. The
powerful trees seem to block efforts to leave and strive to trip, catch, and
snag all who try to travel through. Overhead, wind-tossed branches flex down
from the black, continuous canopy with each gust.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The tall trees are thick and robust here in Mirkwood Forest. They emanate a
sinister power that disdains all non-vegetative life. Creaking and cracking,
limbs and boughs seem to vibrate and rustle at the slightest disturbance, giving
rise to the feeling that perhaps some form of intelligence persists within the
Forest.
name: Deep in Mirkwood

desc:
Colossal, twisted trees hover over one another, composing a dreadful pattern
with their interwoven branches and foliage. Gnarled, fungi-clad branches and
roots encompass the surrounding, spreading a smell of death and decay. The vile
forest stretches its darkness across the landscape, allowing for few easily
accessible spaces. With some effort the area may be further explored in the
east. In the south Mirkwood opens up onto a minor trail.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The corrupted forest displays a scene of twisted trees, bending over one another
in agony. Mossy roots, gnarled and withered, create a treacherous environment,
demanding careful navigation through holes and pits. Periwinkle and ground ivy
grow into a frantic meshwork in the east, blocking any possibility of further
exploration, while in the north small trails and narrow alleys between the trees
allow for investigation.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
The trees ensnare each other with their vast branches and roots, purposefully
creating an almost-impenetrable mesh of vegetation. Many great trees have fallen
heavily to the ground, and are now festering with termites and centipedes. A
faint mist spreads across the forest floor. Traces of strife mark the trunks of
the ancient trees and thorny bushes; but despite being scorched by fire and
wounded by axes and arrows, the forest still stands strong. Dark and dreary
Mirkwood lies open to further exploration to the east and south, while in the
north it turns into an eerie mixture of undergrowth and decay.
name: Murky Forest

desc:
Great roots arch a few feet above the ground, partly covered with poison ivy and
an assortment of mosses. Thorny bushes grow from the damp ground, surrounded by
decomposing foliage. A faint breeze blows through the twisted trees, spreading a
thin mist across the floor. The woods appear to be in deep agony, their
distorted shapes and wounded trunks witness to a recent onslaught. The trees
plunge into a tall thicket in the west, while in the east vines and tangles
smother much of the tall tree trunks. In the south, withered forest passes into
less dense terrain.
name: Eerie Forest

desc:
Many trees gape open with vicious wounds, a clear reminder of the evil that
resides within Mirkwood. Fallen vegetation lies decomposed, scattered among the
younger flora, partly covered in ivy, fungi and mosses. Beech and ash create a
dark, grey surrounding. The trees appear to hover over you, closely watching
every move. The forest expands further into the north and south, closely guarded
by the tall shadows of the attentive trees.
name: Eerie Forest

desc:
Cobweb-covered dark oaks are the dominant species in this area, where a long,
hollowed-out tree-trunk is lying on the ground. The canopies above appear
infested with silky, lead-grey webs. To the west, the forest opens up onto what
looks like a disused, grass-covered roadway.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The grey-coloured forest seems to lack vitality and strength here. Several
once-robust trees, now withered, twist as though in misery. Above and around the
trees blasted bushes and shrub remnants scrabble for survival in this devastated
landscape, completely covered in huge, sticky spider webs. Tree branches and a
few cocoons hang down from the intact canopy, seeming to grab anything that
moves under them.
name: Miserable Trees

desc:
Penetration deeper into Mirkwood has become extremely difficult as every bush
and vine sprouts long and sharp thorns that pierce flesh and snag clothing. The
protruding roots of gnarled trees add to the challenge of making it through
unscathed.
name: Dark, Thorny Woods

desc:
Like guardians at the entrance to a royal tomb, the heavy oaks and thick birches
grow in a dense circle as if preventing one from wandering any further. Their
criss-crossed branches angle down to overlay each other like crossed spears and
gnarled roots have joined with bristly bushes to block the way.
name: Black, Forbidding Forest

desc:
Here, a mass of vegetation has grown up into the forest canopy, and, supported
by the massive branches, now forms a narrow pathway which leads upwards and
eastwards. Vile-looking, old trees loom above the lower vegetation, casting the
forest into a pitch-dark blackness. Massive amounts of small vermin are crawling
around here, feasting on weakened trees and shrubberies. The pale-yellow, warped
roots and vines create a very unwelcoming surrounding, and the options for going
forward are limited to going south, or venturing up along the packed, dense
vegetation.
name: Tangled Forest

desc:
Tangled branches, thick vines and thorny shrubs form a tangled slope here,
creating a tilted path downwards to the west, and upwards to the east.
Underneath and above, decomposing, water-logged, brown foliage serves to hold
the unsteady formation in place. Fungi and mosses thrive down below at ground
level, and thorny bushes makes passage in other directions impossible.
name: Vines and Briars

desc:
The withered forest has grown particularly dense here. Vines and common ivy
ensnare whole trees, wrapping themselves around the trunks in a suffocating
embrace. The leafy branches have grown so closely intertwined as to form a kind
of platform in the forest canopy, high above the forest floor. The damp air
renders the boughs and branches slippery, making footing here far from sure. In
fact, the whole structure seems decidedly unstable.
name: Creaking Forest Canopy

desc:
Massive, vine-ensnared trees surround this forest glade on all sides but to the
south. The site itself appears to be the home of someone or something that does
not care about tidiness or organisation. Dirty cots have been haphazardly hung
from branches and vines, and shards of broken pottery litter the ground. In the
centre of the glade, a number of thick, smoking logs are burning on a huge fire,
making the air soot-ridden and difficult to breathe. Various items used for
cooking and drinking have been thrown around the fire as if whoever tossed them
there did not care for them anymore, and did not mind if they were destroyed. At
the south-eastern corner of the space, an eight foot, carved pole has been
erected.
name: Unwholesome Encampment

desc:
The ground is hard and lacks any real nourishment. The darkened soil cracks open
in places where great roots pop out. Despite the sad state of the soil, there
are great bushes and thorns growing, oak and beech standing tall, all of which
engulf the forest in darkness. The beech and oak line up, creating massive walls
in the west and east. To the south, among an incredible mass of vines, brush and
decomposed vegetation lie the remains of a completely dilapidated dwelling.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Withered by the passage of time, the forest looms in its perpetual state of
darkness. The tree crowns subtly shift, uneasily following the inexorable winds.
In places, great branches have fallen down and become submerged, rotting under
mosses and thorny bushes. Some trees have fallen completely, creating large
craters where their roots once sought nourishment from the ground. Vines and
roots make up a lot of the flora, hanging down from boughs, dipping into bushes
and old stumps. The forest seems to stretch far in all directions, but it is
hard to see through the dense forest.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
This stand of ancient beech and oak once thrived on the nutritious soil of the
great Greenwood, but in this age the once proud and healthy trees are struggling
mightily against each other. With trees grappling with each other and everything
around them with great, massive roots and vines, it seems like a war more than a
forest. Their vast crowns blot out most of the sky, leaving the forest floor in
perpetual shadows. Vines and ivy grow on a few fallen specimens, as well as
hanging in rotting heaps from boughs and trunks. The forest seems to continue
endlessly in all directions.
name: Ancient Mirkwood

desc:
A range of different trees make up this densely packed forest: the crowns of
beech, birch, ash and the odd oak combine to create a densely packed canopy
above. The trees all look old and withered, some with apparent scars from axes.
The forest floor is heavily layered with a myriad of shrubbery, thorns and
periwinkles.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The forest slopes down and inwards, creating a large pit full of thorny briars,
bushes, vines and undergrowth. Gnarled and twisted trees lean in towards each
other, interweaving their huge boughs into a dense canopy above. Poison ivy
encircles some of the trees and branches, drooping down close to the forest
floor like a lifeless strand of web.
name: Dead End

desc:
Dense and discoloured old trees stand here like corrupted, menacing sentinels.
Their boughs are reaching towards each other almost as if they are trying to
cooperate in keeping the daylight above away from the ground. Pale, yellow
grasses and blackish, dark-green mosses grow on the spongy ground, making this
marshy forest hard to travel through.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The fir and oak trees here grow tall and majestic, leaning their far-spanning
crowns onto one another. Roots expand out from the bases of the trunks at
certain places, some of which are covered in silky, immaculate spider webs.
Fungi and moss cover most parts of the fallen, decomposing vegetation. Mirkwood
lies dark and reasonably open all around.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
There is an odd mixture of fir, oak and pine growing here. The trees grow
densely together, stretching their trunks and crowns toward the sky. Massive
boughs intertwine to create an immaculate quilt, denying any natural light from
seeping through. Tangled vines and assorted species of ivy have spread across
the forest floor, covering fallen old trees and branches. Mirkwood lies open for
further exploration to the east and west.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Great roots span across the forest floor, intertwining with dense, thorny bushes
and ensnaring tangles of vines, choking everything in sight. Warped, pale boughs
stretch through the air. Vines are wrapped around many of the great tree trunks,
offering good hideouts for nesting or trapping animals. The forest seems to
shift restlessly, almost as if it were alive. The ashen gloom of the surrounding
area expands to the west, while in the east and north Mirkwood stands in
complete darkness.
name: Gloomy Mirkwood

desc:
Thick vines hang down from the gnarled forest ceiling, half-covered by old,
withered moss. A scent of decay is noticeable throughout the forest. A large
cocoon lies at the foot of a great oak, decomposing and seemingly infested with
larvae and tiny gnats. In the east, the forest rises up like a vast wall, making
it completely impossible to travel there, while the other directions offer more
accessible environments.
name: Malicious Forest

desc:
The humidity here is higher than elsewhere in the forest, and the trees appear
even more twisted and sinister-looking. Withered old silky spider webs consume
the canopies of leaves and branches, blotting out the sky above. The ground
slopes gently southwards, where the ground gradually becomes more soggy. A thick
tangle of thorny shrubs blocks all passage north and west of here, while further
travel into Mirkwood is possible to the south and east.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Far above, the vast canopy of leaves and branches encloses the surrounding area
with its dense, suffocating presence. The trees stand intertwined with one
another, their trunks partly covered by dark green moss. Infestations of fungi
grow on old, rotting stumps. Clusters of dead roots lie partly covered beneath
the damp soil. There is a powerful odour of decay in the air. Mirkwood grows
even denser to the west, while to the east and north the forest opens up for
further exploration.
name: Malicious Forest

desc:
Perversely twisted, dark trees are growing increasingly thickly here. The ground
slopes abruptly to the south, creating a horse-shoe shaped bowl lined with
thorny, impassable shrubs and man-high bushes. The only way out of here seems to
be to the south, further into the darkness of Mirkwood.
name: Dark, Gloomy Forest

desc:
Great dark trees reach far up into the air, their immense canopies intertwining
into a dense quilt that blocks out all sunlight. The dark trees are shifting
into a shade of grey, with silvery spider webs covering large chunks of the tree
trunks. Large bushes and thorns grow here in dense clusters, making the terrain
difficult to travel through. The forest opens up onto narrow natural pathways to
the south and east.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Life has been completely sucked out of the flora here. Great branches and fallen
shrubbery lie decomposed across the forest floor. The scent of death spreads
around the forest. Great webs span across the tree crowns, some of which create
immaculate patterns, others half-broken and abandoned. The withered forest
creates narrow, north-south bound alleys, while to the east Mirkwood refuses
anyone further exploration.
name: Ashen Mirkwood

desc:
Beech and oak rise tall in the north, creating a virtually impenetrable wall.
The path is slowly making its way through the darkened vegetation, leaving few
open routes to travel. A great tree can be seen in the distance to the west,
while in the south and east a veil of darkness spreads across the woods. Looming
over the trail, Mirkwood stands tall and dark in the south, leaving no passage
to reach the meadows beyond the forest borders.
name: A Path along the Forest Wall

desc:
Two enormous beeches have grown into each other, living in symbiosis. Divided
only at the base, they create a large underground cavern, protected from rain
and wind. Their bark is darkened and withered from old age, with patches of
light grey moss covering it. Malicious fungi infest parts of the old, brittle
roots, adding to the intense, foul smell which spreads across the air. The
never-ending darkness of Mirkwood appears less stifling here. The trail running
through this natural cave continues its path to the east and west.
name: A Trail through a Great Tree

desc:
The tree on whose crown you now perch rises far towards the sky, overlooking its
neighbours of oak and beech. Stretching towards the horizon to the south the
landscape is one of vast plains and brush, while to the north, west and east the
dense forest of Mirkwood forms a great quilt of leaves. Great, gnarled branches
offer good support here.
name: Atop the Crowns of the Trees

desc:
Not very long ago, perhaps less than a year, a ferocious fire burned off the
oaks here, leaving behind tall, skeletal remains that jut up against the sky
above like blackened spears. The burned area continues to the north, while in
all other directions tall, black oak and beech trees obscure the view.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
A steep and rocky hillside rises up to the south and east, blocking further
exploration. Approaching from the north, the beech and oak grow dense, engulfing
the entire area in darkness. A sombre mist spreads across the forest floor,
making it even more difficult to discern any details. To the west great beeches
stand united to form a strong barrier, some of which has been marked by fire.
Wounded and withered, Mirkwood still holds together. The ground is littered with
fallen branches. Stripped of leaves and bark they have been left to the
infestation of small malicious bugs. The ensnaring vegetation make travelling
yet more arduous.
name: Deep inside the Darkness

desc:
Great oaks rise toward the sky, intermingling their crowns with the beech and
birch. The fine quilt made up from the leaves far above efficiently blocks out
most natural light. Trunks have fallen to the ground, as if forced away by their
younger offspring. Rotten and decomposed, they now serve as homes for a myriad
of parasites. In the east, south and west Mirkwood spreads its darkness, while
in the north the woods grow even more dense, indeed impenetrable.
name: A Shaded Forest

desc:
The forest floor is cast in shadow by the roots and vines rising from the ground
and encircling the trees. Oak and beech dominate the forest here, their mighty
crowns casting shade over everything. In the east the vegetation appears
distorted and seems to have been viciously ravaged, while in the west the
darkness engulfs the forest completely. An entrance into a pitch-black grove
opens up to the south, just in between two great trees.
name: A Shaded Forest

desc:
The vicious-looking trees lean in to one another, guarding each other's backs
from intruders. Mirkwood stands twisted and tall, engulfing the vegetation in an
immaculate veil of darkness. The beech and oak leave a passage open to the east
and south, ensnaring the heavy underbrush with their roots and vines.
name: A Shaded Forest

desc:
Gnarled branches have fallen to the ground, left dehydrated and brittle by an
old fire. A few majestic trees still stand strong, with their crowns reaching
for the sky, and their trunks forever marked with burns and deep cuts.
Vine-entangled roots rise from the murky soil, offering new foundations for ivy
and periwinkle. In the north Mirkwood lies engulfed in shadow while in the west
it bears the marks of long-forgotten fires. Far below, down an uneven slope, an
old forest trail can be discerned to the south.
name: A Shaded Forest

desc:
Great branches from the ancient trees lie spread across the forest floor, broken
under the twisted, scorched tree trunks. Stout young birch and fir have already
grown dense since the fire, and are already well on the way to restoring the
darkness of Mirkwood. The forest with its young trees expands into the east and
north. A steep hillside leads further west into a more heavily-wooded part of
Mirkwood.
name: A Scorched Forest

desc:
Trees, scorched to their core, have fallen heavily to the ground. However, the
still standing giants of the forest, despite their gaping wounds and obvious
marks of fire, still manage to embrace the area in darkness. Light vegetation
has slowly started to take over from the incinerated trees. The disfigured
forest expands far into the south, while in the east a tall mound of rocks and
boulders appears to have protected the remaining forest from the fire.
name: A Scorched Forest

desc:
Oak and beech dominate the forest here, their mighty crowns casting shade over
everything. A small space free of trees opens up centrally in the area, engulfed
in darkness. Roots and vines, all covered in a shimmering web of spider's silk,
are everywhere. Abandoned cocoons lie scattered across the floor. To the north
darkness lies solid, while to the south there is a hint of rocks and stones.
name: Spidery Grove

desc:
Huge, gnarled trees grow tall and wide, yet seem as though in despair, with
heavily wounded trunks and branches. Many oak and beech have been ripped out of
the ground, leaving their roots to rot and decay as they lie. The undergrowth
rises strong in the east, while in the south the befouled forest lies suffering.
name: Ravaged Mirkwood

desc:
The ground slopes continuously upwards to the east. The towering trees grow very
close together, creating a dark canopy which obscures the sky with a continuous
blanket of interwoven branches and vines. Only a few open paths are available
through the surroundings, as thick-threaded and sticky-looking spider webs have
been spun everywhere: from branch to branch, from one trunk to another, and from
the ground to the upper canopy.
name: Dark Forested Hills

desc:
Several trees have broken in half to the west, the broken-off trunks piling up
into a tall, stout barrier. The many fallen beech and oak here have been
overgrown with thorny bushes and vines. Zigzagging between the roots of those
trees still standing are long columns of various insects, making their way to
and from the decomposing debris. The vegetation hinders further exploration to
the south and west, while in the north the despair which is Mirkwood continues.
name: Ravaged Mirkwood

desc:
Here, gloomy walls of trees form a narrow passage which runs approximately north
to south inside Mirkwood. On this path, two leafless, skeletal trees stand
alone; their shapes resemble a strange mixture of tree and man. Around and above
the open area, dense vegetation and thick tree trunks block travel in any other
direction.
name: Dark, Gloomy Forest

desc:
Large and gloomy oak trees dominate the flora here, growing close together and
creating a sombre canopy above. A few white stones have been piled up here,
almost acting as some kind of landmark. Maybe it was set there to help weary
travellers to find the direction of the road, which can be seen between the
trees. North of here, the trees grow even thicker, and the darkness makes it
hard to see very far.
name: Dark, Gloomy Forest

desc:
Great ashen-grey boughs hang down above this relatively clear patch of forest.
Further north, the trees grow tighter together, intermingling their branches
with each other. Between the thick trees to the south, some kind of flat,
treeless area can be spied.
name: Thin Forest

desc:
The dark forest spreads out in all directions. All kinds of shrouded, indistinct
oak trees have grown into different shapes and forms; some of them are badly
twisted, as if something eldrich influenced their growth. Patches of bare soil
can be seen through the leaf-litter and moss that cover the ground. A few large
webs are stretched between the oaks to the east and south.
name: Tormented Forest

desc:
The trees that cover these darkened, rolling hills are badly twisted, as if some
unfathomable source of evil has caused them great pain. The bark on the looming
trunks is black and infested with green and grey fungi. The dense canopy above
is also darker than one might expect of a forest; the leaves are a dark shade of
green and seem unusually thick.
name: Dark, Forested Hills

desc:
A lot of the trees here are covered by large spider webs. A dried-out cocoon
made out of silky web is lying next to a tree, covered with a mass of maze-like
webs. It is impossible to discern the sky through the canopy overhead, because
the trees here are growing far too densely for the outside world to be seen.
name: Webbed Forest

desc:
These lands are populated by numerous dark, twisted trees. The ground mostly
consists of decaying leaves and rotting stumps of old trees. The forest of
Mirkwood continues in all directions, and to the west the forest floor rises
into low hills. Massive strands of what look like old, decaying cobwebs are
hanging from the leafy canopy above.
name: Dark Gloomy Forest

desc:
The trees of the forest are growing very close to each other here, their trunks
look swarthy and dark as though infected with a contagion. An unusual number of
dead and rotting ant hills litter the ground, discarded decrepit husks of
colonies long since extinct. The only obvious route of travel through Mirkwood
here is towards the west, where the density of brooding trees and tangled
undergrowth is the least obstructive.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
Among these defiled lands, great maze-like spider webs cover almost everything
in sight. The mesh-like, silky webs reach from the corrupted grass bed to the
degenerate treetops, creating pathways of silk that hang between the warped
trees. These thick cobwebs combined with the dark, smothering trees prevent any
light from penetrating all the way to the ground, making it difficult to scout
ahead in any direction.
name: Webbed Forest

desc:
Murky, hollowed-out oak trees stand here closely together like a legion of great
black grotesque statues. At ground level, they are blackened and rotting, but
further up into the canopy small green leaves can be seen straining upwards
towards the unseen sky above. Great fly-infested cobwebs hang between the
trunks, they appear to be as big as fishing nets.
name: A Legion of Dead Oaks

desc:
Occasional faint sounds originate from somewhere in the tree tops above,
scraping and rustling noises unlike those that branches do when the wind move
them against each other. Silky webs connect the trees above at canopy level,
creating what looks like pathways between them. The bark on the trees looks dead
and rotting, as if the silky webs have sucked all normal life from it.
name: Trees at the Edge of the Road

desc:
A noticeably-large oak tree elevates itself from the surrounding, smaller trees.
While its majestic size is breathtaking, the tree itself appears to be befouled
by a ghastly sickness. Its once royal branches and imposing trunk are clad in
silken webs, and the last leaves are slowly dying. Falling for the last time,
the final act is to nourish the encircling vegetation.
name: Webbed Tree

desc:
Unnaturally dark oaks grow in bizarre, twisted shapes here. The leaves of their
crowns appear dead and withered, yet they still manage to block out any view of
the sky above. At ground level black soil, mud, stones and patches of
corpse-yellow grass are all covered indiscriminately by a fine mesh of old,
rotting cobwebs studded with the corpses of uncountable dead moths.
name: Twisted Mirkwood

desc:
Sombre grasses cover the many fallen branches that blanket the ground.
Dreary-looking trees grow towards a dark canopy of leaves and patches of spider
web. A few baby spiders can be spied moving tirelessly along the tree-trunks
south of here. Eastwards the ground starts a gentle ascent, while the southern
lands seems to descend further and further into the great dark of Mirkwood.
name: Weary Forest

desc:
Thick oaks grow sparsely here on a slightly ascending bed of leaves and
overgrown grass. To the south, the oaks appear to be darker and more
corrupted-looking; and their gnarled, bone-white aerial roots are growing
towards the more healthy-looking brethren. A few of the fairer oaks have had
their trunks embraced by roots from the southern trees, and it certainly appears
like the forest is trying to spread whatever plague that torments it. The canopy
above shifts from lush green colours up here, to dark, rusty, dead tones further
south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Smaller oaks grow here among dense bushes and thorny shrubs, a combination that
serves to completely obscure the ground. A large stone, shaped by decades of
wind and rain creates a shelter-like structure towards the south, where the
broken and rotting shaft of some kind of weapon has been left next to what looks
like an old, forgotten campsite. Scant patches of moss decorate the stone in
shades of grey and green.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Cobwebs are spreading like a plague in this area, consuming trees, stones and
bushes. Skulls and bones from small animals and man-like creatures litter the
ground, and broken shields and shafts of weapons lie scattered among them. A few
silken cocoons are swaying from the low-hanging boughs that stretch out from the
tree-crowns. To the west a large tree can be distinguished, while further to the
east, a few trees looks like they have been burned by fire.
name: Webbed Forest

desc:
Large, dark oaks grow tightly together here, their great crowns completely
blocking out the sky above. Examining the trunks reveals that a forest fire once
scorched these trees. Black soot stains reach along the trunks, almost looking
like black shadows creeping up towards the sky along the dark brown bark.
name: Burned Forest

desc:
Great sturdy dark-leaved oaks grow tightly together, creating a terrain that is
very hard to travel through. Dense shrubberies with sad-looking, withered leaves
grow tightly beneath them, sucking nutrients out of the rotting, muddy soil. A
few large boulders can be spied between the trunks to the south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Here, the terrain consists of overgrown bushes and dense oaks, great old trees
whose roots have grown bizarrely large, growing outwards from the trunk twenty
feet in every direction. Some of the aerial roots are growing upwards, ensnaring
the trees around them in a suffocating embrace.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Twisted, dense oaks grow here, their great branches grasping for space and
suffocating all other growth around them. The boughs above are so densely
interwoven that no sign of the sky can be discerned through them. North of here,
the ground looks water-logged, while what could be a road lies to the south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The fire has completely devastated this part of the forest. A blackened glade
with a few charred tree trunks is all that remains of the forest that is looming
around the opening, as dark and oppressive as ever. New undergrowth has started
to grow from the ashes: short grass and a few short plants grow on the ground.
name: A Blackened Glade

desc:
Here, the tree-crowns above are covered by silken cobwebs with long, elegant
threads hanging down from the branches. The ground is littered by skeletons of
small animals and dead, decomposing spiders. Great sticky walls of web stretch
between the trunks of the forest, creating impassable obstructions.
name: Web-Infested Forest

desc:
A few tall hills dominate the scenery here, rising over the nearby forest floor
like a row of darkened, rotting teeth. The hills are fully covered by the
blackened, ancient trees of Mirkwood; the trees make passage on the slopes
tiring, and severely limit visibility. The ground is strewn with mossy boulders,
black weeds and a few dry, dead bushes.
name: Dark Hills inside Mirkwood

desc:
A dark, thick forest covers a range of jagged, blackened hills that rise above
the forest floor. Sinister oaks grow in abundance in this area; some of them are
dried-out and leafless, while others are huge, thick and lush. Boulders litter
the ground, set between patches of cracked, dried-up mud and small, thorny
bushes.
name: Forested, Jagged Hills

desc:
This large, swamp-like bog makes the ground in this area treacherous and hard to
navigate. However, it is possible to get around by staying close to the trees
that grow here on the spongy, grass-covered islets that jut out of the sodden,
reeking ground. Walking is difficult here, riding is impossible.
name: Stinking Bog

desc:
The thick black trunks of long-dead beech trees still stand here in this murky,
water-logged bog. Here and there, strange mushrooms and unusual-looking plants
grow in disproportionate patterns. A small path, marked by yellowish rocks,
snakes through the area; however it is unclear what the purpose of the trail
could be.
name: Black Morass

desc:
A lot of large, thick oaks grow here. Their root systems are partially above
ground, making passage difficult and strenuous. At the base of some of the
trees, great chafing marks are visible, and it looks as if some large animal has
used the trees to sharpen its tusks or horns.
name: Tangled Roots

desc:
The terrain slopes up and down, following the shape of heavily forested hills.
The travelling is hard as rock formations punch upwards between the huge tree
trunks like granite fists, and numerous moss-covered roots twist on the ground
as if trying to trip, grab and trap anyone coming too close to them. Far above,
the branches and vines have entangled into each other forming a dense canopy.
name: Great Jumble of Rocks under the Black Trees

desc:
Tall, craggy hills rise all around, leaving a long but narrow valley between
them. The hillsides to the north and south are covered by thick trees, entangled
shrubbery and occasional sharp-edged boulders. Uncountable branches meet each
other in layer upon layer, almost completely covering and shadowing the
tunnel-like pathway on the ground level.
name: Forested Pass between the Craggy Hills

desc:
An intact canopy of Mirkwood covers a tall hill which rises above the
surrounding ground. Black trees stand like towers and in the darkness below them
continuous shrubbery, rock boulders and small stony ridges fill the landscape.
The slopes of the hill are descending relatively calmly making travelling
possible although potentially treacherous.
name: A Dark, Rock-strewn Hill

desc:
The ground level of the forest slopes up and down in a random fashion, making
movement possible only with great care. The area is filled with black pine trees
and grey, crumbly rock. The canopy continues in all directions, filtering and
blocking out most of the light. There are especially steep and rocky hillsides
to the east and south.
name: Rocky, Forested Slopes

desc:
Dark trees and lush, black bushes cover the terrain which starts to angle
sharply upwards to the north, and to sink abruptly to the south. Down the slope,
the trees get even thicker and crowd together, blocking the view further on. Up
the slope, a turf war is being fought between vegetation and rock with neither
side winning.
name: Forested Slope

desc:
Breaking away from the northern slopes is a series of heavily forested hills.
Steeply sloping ground, gigantic trees and occasional boulders and moss-covered
loose rocks make the terrain hard to travel. Taken over by thick brush and dark
trees, all directions save north are quite impassable.
name: Overgrown Hills

desc:
The scent of musty trees and rank vegetation is overpowering in this dark and
thick grove. The whole area has been wrapped into a thick layer of silvery,
sticky-looking spider webs. A dozen grotesque cocoons dangle from the branches,
like hanged men on the gallows. Some of the cocoons seem to be moving
occasionally.
name: Spiders' Nest

desc:
Large stands of birch and pine seem to whisper to each other, each and every
tree being woven into a huge network of spider webs which covers the whole area.
Above, gangways made of spider silk reach from one tree top to another, creating
a network of passageways within the canopy. On ground level, shrubs of all sizes
hug the trunks.
name: Network of Webs

desc:
Serving as a transition between the rising hills and the deep forest, this swell
in the landscape is dotted with pine and birch trees. Small boulders are planted
here and there in the loamy soil. Between the rocks, pine needles and decaying
leaves litter the ground.
name: A Forested Rise

desc:
Pale spider webs have been woven between the old gnarled trees whose dark
branches hang over the ground as if poised to grab unsuspecting travellers
below. The ground is littered with broken branches and ancient, rotting trunks
mixed with heavy mosses. Above, the continuous dense canopy continues to all
directions totally blocking the view to the sky.
name: Webbed Woods

desc:
The uneasy presence that permeates Mirkwood is very strong here. Towering pines
and birch jostle with ancient oak, all covered with an intact, endless-looking
wrapping of old cobwebs. With any passing breeze, the forest seems to come alive
with a chorus of whispers as if a great, quiet discussion was going on. Gnarled
roots mixed with heavy shrubs and bushes make the way tough.
name: Cobweb-covered Forest

desc:
The sloping hillside here is festooned with trim fir and birch trees. Nestled
between the trunks of the trees are rock protrusions, popping out of the ground
in random places. Long beards of moss hang off slender branches to drape the
ground in a luxuriously soft carpet. Evidence of birds and other small animals
can be seen near the forest's edge.
name: Forested Slope

desc:
A dense, shady forest dips and rises in a somewhat undulating manner. Fallen
trees from long ago lean against standing trunks like old men tired of living.
Dappled leaves and verdant mosses cloak almost every surface while the fungi try
to take over from below.
name: A Rolling Forest

desc:
Tension fills the air in this dark, heavily-wooded area. The towering trees seem
to be whispering to each other every time a leaf rustles or a branch creaks
noisily. Thick, sticky spider webs have been woven to the branches and between
the trunks, creating traps for careless travellers. Animal noises are few and
far between although the way here is crowded with dense shrubs and thick bushes
that scrape and catch.
name: Foreboding Woods

desc:
Here, the ground slopes downwards to the south, where some kind of overgrown
roadway can be glimpsed in between the dark-barked oak trunks. Between two
enormous, moss-covered trunks lie the remains of what once must have been a hut
of some kind; all that remains of it now are a few rotting planks that used to
form its walls. A musty smell permeates the area, hinting that particularly
pungent mushrooms grow nearby.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
A black morass, with high reeds and shallow, opaque water forms north of here,
where large oaks and beeches grow out of small, grassy islands. The ground here
is covered by mud and a few strangely pale plants. A large stone stands next to
one of the oak trees here, overgrown with black and red lichen and moss.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Tree-trunks stand shoulder to shoulder in aggressive poses as the way becomes
tight and close. A menacing feeling of intelligent awareness is present among
the woods and intensifies deeper inside. Angry boughs shake thorny branches at
whoever passes underneath and the wildlife seems almost scared to scurry around.
name: Menacing Stand of Trees

desc:
Aside from the heavy foliage, few living things dare to penetrate this deep into
Mirkwood. The menacing presence of the forest is almost overpowering and every
root, twig, and branch tries to catch and snag anything that moves of its own
accord. The forest is disturbingly quiet here.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
Putrescent fungi and grey spider webs stick like a thick spackle on the sides of
dark trees that have been twisted and gnarled to resemble angry old men, hunched
over and yelling. Glistening knots give faces to the menacing trees while lurid
dangling mosses drape thick outstretched branches like a fur stole.
name: Dark, Webbed Woods

desc:
Vile trees reach out with their branches, grasping at the crowns of their
neighbouring trees to create a vast canopy of leaves. The ground ivy thoroughly
engulfs the roots rising up from the earth. The carcass of a bear lies rotting
below a great oak, completely covered with flies, worms and other pests. The
forest expands to the north, while the terrain seems cumbersome in the east and
south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Rotting vines, leaves and fallen tree trunks lie spread across muddy ground,
filling the air with a scent of decay. The poison ivy reaches out from the
vile-looking trees which stand hunched over one another with their twisted,
gnarled trunks and branches. The forest is pitch-black, and it is difficult to
discern what lies beyond the immediate surroundings.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The dark forest guards itself closely from intruders with its densely-growing
vines, dark ferns and thorny thickets. Some of the great trees have fallen
heavily to the ground and now house a myriad of pests. The gnarled trees spread
their territory far into the north and east, leaving no vegetation untouched by
its corrupting darkness.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Corrupted trees, ensnared by darkened vines, have fallen heavily to the ground.
There are obvious traces of strife here, with scattered bones, rusted shields
and axes still jammed into stumps across the forest floor. The vile surroundings
stretch further into the west and south.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Vines and poison ivy are aggressively approaching the ground, hanging down from
the trees which support them. Roots are crossing each other's paths, creating
small pits for thorny bushes, as well as small animal hideouts. A great earth
mound rises to the north, leaving a small passage that passes just east of it.
To the extreme east gnarled Mirkwood spreads its canopy crowns high and far.
Great shadows encompass the forest, blotting out any specific details of the
land to the south.
name: Inside a Malicious Forest

desc:
The trees of Mirkwood grow as dense as ever at the bottom of this depression,
with forested slopes stretching upwards to the east and west. A sad moaning can
be heard from within the trees, whose crowns sway restlessly back and forth, yet
keep their interwoven forest roof intact. Shrubs grow densely between the vines,
which entangle each other in a complex web-like formation. Darkness engulfs the
surroundings, demanding attentiveness from the average traveller.
name: Deep inside Mirkwood

desc:
The forest starts to gather itself slightly. Its darkened trees and heavy
vegetation grow more and more dense. Great roots extend from the dark soil,
spanning far across the forest floor, only to dig into the ground again.
Darkness enshrouds Mirkwood, making it difficult to discern anything other than
the immediate surroundings. Old, fallen vegetation gives nourishment to every
growing thing, expanding and weaving underneath and around the warped roots and
trees on the forest floor.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Misshapen and twisted trees with great branches and roots form a difficult area
to travel through. Although relatively spread out, the thick clusters of plants
that span the forest floor require caution as to where to put ones feet. Some of
the trees here suffer from cleaved trunk and broken boughs, as if someone had
taken an axe to them in the recent past. The dark and malicious forest opens up
for further exploration in the east and west, while it looks perfectly
impassable to the north and south.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
The dense forest is full of gnarled and twisted trees that are engulfing the
surroundings in the pitch-black darkness. The soil beneath has become quite soft
allowing new shrubs and roots to take hold more easily. Some of the great oaks
and beeches have fallen down; slowly decomposing they are now bringing a
perpetual smell of rot and decay to the air.
name: Dark, Twisted Forest

desc:
The trees of this forest rise tall, stretching up towards the sky. The great
tree crowns unite up there, forming a tightly interwoven quilt that keeps the
forest in eternal darkness. The majority of the trees look agonized, their
trunks warped and their branches twisted. Some have been cleaved in the middle
with axes, while others appear to have been struck by lightning at some point.
Vines and ivy smother most of the many fallen trees that litter the forest
floor. The route to the south appears impossible to take, as a wall of tangled,
thorny shrubs and gnarled trees rise up to form a formidable wall. The east and
west offer more room for further travelling.
name: Dark, Twisted Forest

desc:
This corrupted forest appears to stretch far away in all directions. The tree
crowns above intertwine, creating a dense and impenentrable quilt. At ground
level, the blackened soil is grainy, greasy and somewhat soft to the touch.
Fallen trees have left great craters behind, from where their roots used to dig
deep into the forest floor. Vines and ivy cover most of the fallen trunks, and
small centipedes, beetles and various other insects crawl around on the ground,
feasting on dead and decomposing plant materials as well as on each other.
name: Dark, Twisted Forest

desc:
Great, warped roots spring out from the ground, criss-crossing the forest floor
and digging into the dirt with gusto. The gnarled trunks twist and turn in a
most unnatural manner. Heavily-intertwined branches and boughs create a natural
patchwork that leaves the forest in a pitch-black darkness. The dark soil is
quite soft and saturated with water, giving off an unmistakable smell of rot and
decay. An immense tangle of vegetations rise in the south, leaving the area
impossible to travel through. The warped forest looks slightly more open to the
west.
name: Dark, Twisted Forest

desc:
Warped trees lean onto each other for support here. Their heavy crowns grow so
densely together that no sunlight from above can penetrate down to the ground.
The tree-trunks carry open, gaping wounds, while others have been smitten with
some kind of black, lumpy parasitical growth. South of here, an immense tangle
of roots rupture out of the ground. Vines and shrubs grow everywhere in between
the great, malicious-looking trees. The air is quite moist, and the dark soil is
spongy and almost saturated by blackish, foul-smelling water.
name: Dark, Twisted Forest

desc:
The trees are bent under the weight of aeons of unfettered growth, while bushes
and shrubs grow unchecked. Many trees appear twisted and bent as if frozen in a
state of mourning. Laced with oddly shimmering spider webs, weeds and long
branches, the forest is very difficult for all but the smallest creatures to
move easily in.
name: Shimmering Webs in the Sombre Woods

desc:
A fine haze swirls through the air in this part of Mirkwood forest. It has a
peculiar scent that is difficult to place. Moisture clings to clothing but none
of it seems to pool on the ground in drinkable quantities. The very trees seem
to be sucking every drop up before it can puddle.
name: Misty Mirkwood

desc:
Black, huge oaks stand side by side, their great branches reaching upwards,
entangling themselves into the intact canopy that does not allow any sunlight to
reach the forest floor. The haze drifting in from the north thickens here to
become actual fog, that now makes any kind of travel hazardous. Dim and dark
tree-like shapes seem to move with menacing gestures, cloaked by the mist.
name: Foggy Forest

desc:
The trees gather together in a tight circle, forming a deep and dark grove.
Thick gnarled trunks and black branches stick together creating an intact wall
which prevents travelling anywhere but to the west. Nothing but a sinister
canopy can be seen above, blocking any light to reach the grove. Occasional
noises of small animals creep through the underbrush.
name: Dark Grove

desc:
Darkness pervades the sad trees in this part of Mirkwood. Heavy foliage and
thick branches crowd out any hint of the sky above. The weight of the
overhanging vegetation causes the trees to droop, contributing to the feeling of
dreariness so evident throughout the woods.
name: Dark, Dreary Woods

desc:
Silky, lead-grey spider webs cover absolutely everything here. A group of
darkened old beeches have been completely overtaken by arachnid activities. The
ground is soggy and muddy, and patches of moss provide the driest patches to
stand on. Two dead trees have fallen against each other here, and their branches
reinforced by the webbing serve to create a solid opening upwards into the
forest canopy.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
The uneven ground, covered in damp moss and the occasional patch of grass,
undulates around the oak and fir. The trees cast faint shadows in the already
dim light. To the east a trail of broken branches and disturbed moss leads into
the heavy undergrowth.
name: A Ravaged Forest

desc:
Beech and oak stretch their branches far towards the sky, meeting in a great
collaboration of crowns far up in the air which creates a veritable quilt of
leaves, preventing almost all natural light from seeping through. Mirkwood grows
into a great barrier to the south, west and east, creating an impenetrable wall.
The soil is dark and nourishing, giving life to an immense amount of greenery
and insect life. The old forest trail opens up in the north.
name: A Dead End in the Forest

desc:
The earth cave is quite narrow, yet deep and tall. The walls of black soil are
covered partly by fungi and mosses, partly by insects and stains of blood. Small
roots and twigs stick out from the roof and sides, some of which carry rugged
tufts of black hair. A gnawed-on carcass of a bear cub lies among a
neatly-gathered pile of leaves and twigs. Remains of cracked bones lie scattered
across the cave. The ground has been heavily packed, as if walked upon for many
a year.
name: Inside an Earthen Cave

desc:
The forest canopy opens up a bit here, allowing a stream of sunlight to reach
the forest floor below, where yellow, waist-high grass grows in abundance. A
ring of rocks have been placed off to the eastern part of the glade; no doubt
this place was sometimes used as a camp ground. The forest around the glade is
massive and dark. Between the bushes, a web-covered mouth of a tunnel leads
diagonally downwards, and in other directions the thick, thorny bushes makes
passage impossible except to the east.
name: Forest Glade

desc:
Towering, dark oaks stand together in dense rows, their massive and gnarled
canopies blotting out any natural light from above. Long, foul-smelling strands
of lichen hang down from the boughs, and blackish, green-tinged mosses cling to
the huge, scarred trunks. Among the upper canopy, platforms made of spider web
and vegetation connect all the trees of the area together. To the east, a narrow
strip of rich red cloth has been attached to a branch with no obvious intention
or meaning.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
Black, gnarled trees grow side by side, populating these forlorn hills and
shading the sloping ground with their dense canopy. Silvery spider webs have
been spun between the dark branches of the trees, and the uppermost threads
disappear into the dark leaves above. A broken crow's nest is lying beneath one
of the trees, and a few cracked and rotten eggs lie next to it. Southwards the
ground turns to mud.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
This dark and murky forest is comprised of blackened oak trees growing on a
craggy, underbrush-covered slope that descends westwards into a swampy, foul-
smelling bog. Movement is very difficult as the ground is covered with stumps,
vines, bushes and other tangled vegetation. A heap of branches, twigs and leaves
has been piled against one of the oak trees; it looks like someone prepared a
huge bonfire but never lit it.
name: Blackened Oaks

desc:
Sloping down from the tall hills to the north, this gentle incline is covered in
vegetation. Thick beech and birch jostle with each other for the light while
massive oak and maple ponder the passage of time. The ground level is relatively
free from forest litter, only some vines are hanging from the canopy.
name: Forested Incline

desc:
Here, the ridges and hills rise high making the terrain hard to travel. A
continuous mass of small bushes with needle-sharp leaves grows on the slopes,
surrounding the massive tree trunks and occasional boulders and rock walls. The
sloping ground continues to all directions but to the south where the ground
seems to be steadily descending down.
name: Hills of Mirkwood

desc:
The continuous canopy of Mirkwood covers the ground, leaving everything into the
dark shadows. Underneath the canopy bushes, vines, and trees fight for space in
the densely packed sinister forest. Broken limbs and rotting stumps add to the
sense of decay and gloom that is ever present in this dank grove.
name: Dank Grove

desc:
Overhanging dark branches push down from above like the huge, muscled arms of
the powerful trees which seem to be glare at any intruder through huge knots and
gnarled bark. The grim trees seem to nod to each other whenever a light breeze
cycles through this oppressive grove. Except for the leaves rustling, all is
strangely quiet.
name: Dark, Forbidding Woods

desc:
A dark grove of mighty oak trees stand in a tight circle leaving a small open
area between their trunks, roots and branches. The trees outside the grove
appear to be shorter and not as thick as the black oaks, but still the forest
seems very dark and tangled. The dense growth of trees subsides only towards the
south where the grove of trees borders the Old Forest Road.
name: Menacing Woods

desc:
A small tunnel has been dug out through the dark soil, just below the great
roots of Mirkwood. Spider webs span across the spaces between the entangling
vines and twigs, trapping small bugs and insects. The tunnel continues quite far
to the west, as well as east. The walls have been reinforced by logs and rope,
and even rock at some places. Dense roots and ivy grow by the entrance and exit
of the dark earth tunnel.
name: A Tunnel under the Forest

desc:
There are obvious traces of a great battle here, with many trees and bushes
showing damage. Something which could be a burial mound rises just at the foot
of a great oak tree. A perfectly-rounded boulder, overgrown with vines and moss,
blocks the way to the north, just below the forest trail. A dreadful stench is
slowly pervading this area, creeping in from the south.
name: Ravaged Mirkwood

desc:
The ground is very uneven here and old, decomposed trees are incorporated into
the heavy undergrowth. The ancient trees rise tall and dark. It appears
difficult to travel further east, up over a small rise, due to the heavily
entangled roots and boughs. Parts of the forest here have been heavily ravaged,
their trunks gaping with open wounds. Animal trails lead into the heavy
undergrowth to the south, while to the west a faint gloom spreads across the
forested hills.
name: A Ravaged Forest

desc:
Malicious-looking periwinkle reaches downwards from withered oak and ash,
intertwining with the foliage and stretching far across the smothering terrain.
A dense canopy hangs far up in the air, efficiently preventing most natural
light from penetrating. Mouldy vines and roots spread over the forest floor.
Fallen beeches serve well as homes for various parasites. The darkness which is
Mirkwood may be further explored to the east and south.
name: Eerie Forest

desc:
The forest rises into a ridge, overlooking the surrounding area. An old forest
trail lies far below, to the north. The oak and beech lean their gnarled
branches against one another, while supporting each other and forming a solid
canopy of leaves, keeping the surroundings dark and dreary. The carcass of a
great horse lies, rotten and pest-infested, close to the northern ridge.
Mirkwood is rather easily accessible to the west, while travelling north looks
both cumbersome and dangerous.
name: A Ridge above the Road

desc:
The walls and ceiling are made out of pitch black soil, tightly packed between
the roots of the great trees outside. Puddles lie scattered over the muddy
floor, nourishing the malicious poison ivy. Loose rocks and forest debris occupy
the south of the cavern, while to the north a passageway opens up behind falling
roots and vines.
name: A Tunnel under the Forest

desc:
The wounded trees of the forest lean their crowns against one other,
collectively supporting the heavy forest roof. The canopy is kept solid by the
dense intertwining of the interweaving vines, boughs and branches. A steep slope
rises to the east while to the west there is a small opening leading into the
darkness.
name: In the Shade of Forested Hills

desc:
You climb up the dangerous-looking steep slope, grabbing support from black
oozing vines. Alas! The treacherous plants twist under your grip, sending you to
an uncontrolled fall down the dark slope. You hit your head into something and
the gruesome sound of a breaking neck is the last thing you register...
name: A Very Dangerous Slope

desc:
The massive beeches have grown into each other, separated only by a modest path
at their bases. The grand crowns sway restlessly on the mossy trunks of the
trees. The path makes its way through Mirkwood forest, slowly zigzagging from
the east and extending its way to the west. Great trees stand tall to the south,
blocking any passage in that direction. Mirkwood forest in all its dreariness
opens up for yet another few leagues to the north.
name: A Path along the Forest Wall

desc:
The woods seem tired and extremely dreary in this dark place. The trunks emanate
a lacklustre energy that inspires nothing and drains anything living. Although
sleep is most unwise here, the heavy haze of dreariness causes the forest's
fatigue to become highly contagious.
name: Dreary Woods

desc:
Time seems to slow in this part of Mirkwood. Majestic firs stand like mighty
kings, attended by maples and hazels. Coating the ground like a royal carpet,
feather mosses intersperse with acid needle droppings to create a rich, organic
fragrance. Twisted vines and fallen branches frame the view in all directions.
name: Primeval Forest
